name: pad3r-public
description: Official implementation of PAD3R, a pipeline for reconstructing dynamic 3D objects from a single casual monocular video by jointly modeling object deformation and camera motion.
readme_summary: PAD3R is a Python-based pipeline for reconstructing dynamic 3D objects from a single casual monocular video. It achieves this by jointly modeling object deformation and camera motion through a three-stage process involving static 3D reconstruction, camera pose estimation, and dynamic 4D reconstruction.
key_features: Reconstructs dynamic 4D objects from single casual monocular videos
Jointly models object deformation and camera motion
Three-stage reconstruction pipeline: Static 3D, PoseNet, Dynamic 4D
Utilizes Zero123 SDS, SuGaR, Gaussian Splatting, DINOv2, and CoTracker3
primary_use_case: Reconstructing dynamic 3D objects from casual monocular videos.
cover_image_prompt: A sculptor in a digital studio, using a single video feed displayed on a large screen as their inspiration. The video shows a person dancing. The sculptor's hands, glowing with ethereal light, are shaping a translucent, evolving 3D model of the dancer that mirrors the movements from the video. Around the studio, subtle holographic projections display wireframes and motion capture data, hinting at the underlying technology. The repository name 'PAD3R' is subtly etched onto a digital tool. The scene is bathed in soft, creative light, with a clean, modern aesthetic. The overall style should be a 3D isometric illustration, emphasizing the transformation from 2D video to dynamic 3D form.

name: SPAG4d
description: Convert 360° equirectangular panoramas into viewable 3D Gaussian Splat files.
readme_summary: SPAG-4D is a Python-based tool that converts 360° equirectangular panoramic images into explorable 3D Gaussian Splat scenes. It offers multiple generator backends for depth estimation and direct Gaussian prediction, with optional refinement steps to fill disocclusions and improve visual quality.
key_features: Conversion of 360° panoramas to 3D Gaussian Splats
Multiple generator backends: DA360, DAP, SHARP 360
Depth-based projection via spherical geometry
Direct Gaussian prediction using Apple's SHARP model
Optional refinement with GSFix3D (diffusion inpainting)
Optional refinement with OmniRoam v2 (trajectory-coherent video generation)
Optional pre-processing upscaling with SeedVR2
Web UI for easy usage
primary_use_case: Transforming panoramic photos into interactive 3D environments using Gaussian Splatting technology.
cover_image_prompt: A magical portal opening in the center of a flat, circular panoramic photograph. From the portal, a vibrant, three-dimensional scene made of glowing, scattered particles (representing Gaussian splats) is emerging and expanding outwards, inviting the viewer to step into it. The original panorama is depicted as a flat, slightly transparent disc with faint grid lines, showing the input. The emerging 3D scene is detailed and colorful, hinting at depth and interactivity. Subtle digital particles and faint code snippets float around the portal's edge, connecting the magic to technology. The overall mood is wondrous and exploratory, with a clean, modern aesthetic. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a focus on clear visual metaphor and luminous details.

name: GSAP
description: GSAP (GreenSock Animation Platform), a JavaScript animation library for the modern web
readme_summary: GSAP (GreenSock Animation Platform) is a high-performance JavaScript animation library for the modern web. It enables developers to create complex animations across all major browsers for various elements like CSS, SVG, and even generic JavaScript objects, with optional plugins for advanced effects like scroll-based animations and motion paths.
key_features: Framework-agnostic JavaScript animation library
High-performance animations
Works in every major browser
Animates CSS, SVG, canvas, React, Vue, WebGL, colors, strings, motion paths, generic objects
ScrollTrigger plugin for scroll-based animations
gsap.matchMedia() for responsive and accessible animations
Zero dependencies
Optional plugins for advanced effects (MorphSVG, SplitText, MotionPathPlugin, Flip, Observer)
CDN and NPM installation options
primary_use_case: Creating high-performance, complex animations for web development across all major browsers.
cover_image_prompt: A master conductor standing on a stage, wielding a glowing baton that directs a symphony of diverse elements like flowing CSS gradients, morphing SVG shapes, and soaring text. The conductor's movements create elegant, synchronized animations that fill the digital stage. Subtle UI elements showing code snippets and animation timelines are integrated into the stage design. The scene is vibrant and dynamic, with a focus on the conductor as the central control point for all animated elements. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, modern aesthetic and a bright, engaging color palette, emphasizing the effortless control and creativity GSAP provides.

name: dragonfly
description: A modern replacement for Redis and Memcached
readme_summary: Dragonfly is a high-performance, in-memory data store designed as a modern replacement for Redis and Memcached. It offers significantly higher throughput and lower latency, requiring no code changes for adoption and utilizing resources more efficiently.
key_features: Full compatibility with Redis and Memcached APIs
25X higher throughput compared to legacy datastores
Lower tail latency and higher cache hit rates
Up to 80% less resource utilization for the same workload
Built for modern application workloads
primary_use_case: To serve as a faster, more efficient, and resource-conscious in-memory data store for applications currently using Redis or Memcached, improving overall performance and reducing operational costs.
cover_image_prompt: A powerful, sleek, futuristic engine humming with energy, effortlessly processing a torrent of data streams (represented by glowing blue and green particles). This engine is clearly labeled 'Dragonfly' and is shown replacing a collection of older, sputtering, steam-powered machines (representing Redis and Memcached). The older machines are visibly struggling to keep up, emitting smoke and sparks, while the Dragonfly engine is smooth and efficient. The background is a clean, modern data center environment with subtle UI elements displaying performance metrics like 'throughput' and 'latency' in a positive, upward trend. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with vibrant, glowing effects and a focus on the contrast between old and new technology.

name: promptfoo
description: Test your prompts, agents, and RAGs. Red teaming/pentesting/vulnerability scanning for AI. Compare performance of GPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama, and more. Simple declarative configs with command line and CI/CD integration.  Used by OpenAI and Anthropic.
readme_summary: Promptfoo is a command-line interface and library designed for testing, evaluating, and red-teaming Large Language Model (LLM) applications. It enables developers to compare the performance of different models, automate checks in CI/CD pipelines, and scan for vulnerabilities to ensure secure and reliable AI app deployment.
key_features: Automated evaluations of prompts and models
Red teaming and vulnerability scanning for LLM apps
Side-by-side model comparison (OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure, Bedrock, Ollama, etc.)
CI/CD integration for automated checks
Code scanning for LLM-related security and compliance
Developer-first features like live reload and caching
Local execution for privacy
primary_use_case: Evaluating and red-teaming LLM applications to ensure their security, reliability, and performance.
cover_image_prompt: A meticulous scientist in a high-tech laboratory, carefully calibrating a complex array of beakers and test tubes, each representing a different LLM model (GPT, Claude, Gemini). The scientist is feeding various 'prompt' ingredients into a central distillation apparatus, which then outputs perfectly formed, glowing 'reliable AI app' outputs into separate, labeled vials. Small screens around the lab display performance metrics and vulnerability scan results. The overall mood is one of precision and discovery, with a clean, modern aesthetic and subtle digital particle effects. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with vibrant, clear colors and sharp details, emphasizing the process of rigorous testing and refinement.

name: Vulkan-Samples
description: One stop solution for all Vulkan samples
readme_summary: Vulkan Samples is a comprehensive collection of C++ resources designed to help developers learn and implement optimized Vulkan applications. It provides API samples for beginners and advanced performance samples demonstrating best practices with real-time profiling.
key_features: Collection of Vulkan API samples for learning
Performance samples demonstrating best practices
Real-time profiling information
Detailed tutorials with in-depth explanations
C++20 requirement
Vulkan 1.1+ device requirement
primary_use_case: Learning and implementing optimized Vulkan applications through practical samples and tutorials.
cover_image_prompt: A master craftsman in a futuristic workshop, meticulously assembling intricate, glowing geometric structures using a blueprint that hovers in the air. The blueprint displays complex code snippets and performance graphs, representing Vulkan API calls and optimizations. The craftsman's tools are stylized digital instruments that emit light particles, transforming raw data streams into polished, vibrant 3D models. The workshop is clean and well-lit, with subtle holographic displays showing real-time performance metrics. The overall scene should evoke a sense of precision, learning, and advanced graphics development. 3D isometric illustration style with a focus on clear visual metaphor and vibrant, glowing elements.
